title: Bifrost Configuration Schema
description: Schema for Bifrost HTTP transport configuration

$schema.description: The schema version. This should be set to "https://www.getbifrost.ai/schema"

version.description: Controls how empty arrays in allow-list fields (models, allowed_models, key_ids, tools_to_execute) are interpreted. Omit or set to 2 for v1.5.0+ semantics: empty = deny all, ["*"] = allow all. Set to 1 to restore v1.4.x semantics: empty = allow all.

encryption_key.description: You can set the value as env.<ENV_VAR_NAME> to use an environment variable. We also read encryption key from BIFROST_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable. Note: once set, the encryption key cannot be changed unless you clean up the database. Accepts any string; a secure 32-byte AES-256 key will be derived using Argon2id KDF. If not provided, data will be saved in plain text. Recommended: use a passphrase of at least 16 bytes for better security

drop_excess_requests.description: Whether to drop excess requests when pool is full

initial_pool_size.description: Initial size of the connection pool

prometheus_labels.description: Labels to use for Prometheus metrics

allowed_origins.description: CORS allowed origins (supports "*" or URI strings)

disable_content_logging.description: Disable logging of sensitive content (inputs, outputs, embeddings, etc.)

disable_db_pings_in_health.description: Disable DB pings in health check

log_retention_days.description: Number of days to retain logs

enforce_auth_on_inference.description: Require auth (VK, API key, or user token) on inference endpoints

max_request_body_size_mb.description: Maximum request body size in MB

compat.description: Compat plugin configuration for request type conversion, parameter dropping, and parameter value conversion

convert_text_to_chat.description: Convert text completion requests to chat for models that only support chat

convert_chat_to_responses.description: Convert chat completion requests to responses for models that only support responses

should_drop_params.description: Drop unsupported parameters based on model catalog allowlist

should_convert_params.description: Converts model parameter values that are not supported by the model.

header_filter_config.description: Global header filtering configuration for x-bf-eh-* headers forwarded to LLM providers

allowlist.description: If non-empty, only these headers (from x-bf-eh-* prefix) are allowed to be forwarded

denylist.description: Headers to always block from being forwarded

async_job_result_ttl.description: Default TTL for async job results in seconds (default: 3600 = 1 hour)

required_headers.description: Headers that must be present on every request. Requests missing any of these headers are rejected with 400. Case-insensitive matching.

logging_headers.description: Headers to capture in log metadata. Values are extracted from incoming requests and stored in the metadata field of log entries.

whitelisted_routes.description: Routes that bypass auth middleware. Requests to these exact paths skip authentication checks.

hide_deleted_virtual_keys_in_filters.description: When true, deleted virtual keys are omitted from logs and MCP logs filter data.

allowed_headers.description: Additional allowed headers for CORS and WebSocket

mcp_agent_depth.description: Maximum depth for MCP agent mode tool execution

mcp_tool_execution_timeout.description: Timeout for individual MCP tool execution in seconds

mcp_code_mode_binding_level.description: Code mode binding level for MCP tools

mcp_tool_sync_interval.description: Global tool sync interval in minutes (0 = disabled)

mcp_disable_auto_tool_inject.description: When true, MCP tools are not automatically injected into requests. Tools are only included when explicitly specified via request context filters or headers, such as x-bf-mcp-include-tools or x-bf-mcp-include-clients.

routing_chain_max_depth.description: Maximum depth for routing rule chain evaluation

governance.description: Governance configuration for budgets, rate limits, customers, teams, virtual keys, and routing rules

virtual_key_id.description: ID of the virtual key this budget belongs to (mutually exclusive with provider_config_id)

provider_config_id.description: ID of the provider config this budget belongs to (mutually exclusive with virtual_key_id)

token_last_reset.description: Last time token counter was reset

request_last_reset.description: Last time request counter was reset

is_active.description: Whether the virtual key is active

calendar_aligned.description: Snap all budget resets to calendar boundaries (day, week, month, year)

team_id.description: Associated team ID (mutually exclusive with customer_id)

customer_id.description: Associated customer ID (mutually exclusive with team_id)

provider_configs.description: Provider configurations for this virtual key (empty means no providers allowed, deny-by-default)

mcp_configs.description: MCP configurations for this virtual key (empty array means no MCP tools allowed, deny-by-default)

routing_rules.description: Routing rules for dynamic provider/model selection based on CEL expressions

pricing_overrides.description: Scoped pricing overrides applied at runtime by the model catalog

model_configs.description: Per-model rate limit and budget configurations

model_name.description: Model name to apply the configuration to

provider.description: Optional provider name to scope this config

budget_id.description: Budget ID to associate with this model

rate_limit_id.description: Rate limit ID to associate with this model

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

tool_sync_interval.description: Global default interval for syncing tools from MCP servers (Go duration, e.g. '10m', '1h')

type.description: Vector store type (use "redis" for Redis or Valkey-compatible endpoints)

password.description: Database password. Leave empty if you want to use IAM role authentication.

max_idle_conns.description: Maximum number of idle connections in the pool (default: 5)

max_open_conns.description: Maximum number of open connections to the database (default: 50)

password.description: Database password. Leave empty if you want to use IAM role authentication.

max_idle_conns.description: Maximum number of idle connections in the pool (default: 5)

max_open_conns.description: Maximum number of open connections to the database (default: 50)

object_storage.description: Optional object storage for offloading log payloads. When configured, large request/response payloads are stored in S3/GCS while the DB keeps only lightweight index data.

bucket.description: Bucket name. Supports env var reference (e.g. env.S3_BUCKET)

prefix.description: Key prefix for stored objects (default: bifrost)

compress.description: Enable gzip compression for stored objects. Default: false

region.description: AWS region. Supports env var reference

endpoint.description: Custom S3-compatible endpoint for MinIO/R2. Supports env var reference

access_key_id.description: AWS access key ID. Omit to use default credential chain (instance role, env vars, etc.). Supports env var reference

secret_access_key.description: AWS secret access key. Supports env var reference

session_token.description: AWS session token for STS temporary credentials. Supports env var reference

role_arn.description: AWS IAM role ARN for STS AssumeRole. Works with static creds or instance role. Supports env var reference

force_path_style.description: Use path-style URLs for S3 (required for MinIO). Default: false

credentials_json.description: GCP service account credentials JSON or file path. Omit to use Application Default Credentials. Supports env var reference

credentials.description: Deprecated: use credentials_json. Kept for backwards compatibility.

project_id.description: GCP project ID override. Supports env var reference

retention_days.description: Days to retain log entries. 0 disables retention-based cleanup.

name.description: Name of the plugin (built-in: telemetry, prompts, logging, governance, maxim, semantic_cache, otel, or custom plugin name)

path.description: Path to the plugin (optional, required for dynamic plugins)

version.description: Version of the plugin (default: 1). Increment in this number will force a reload of the plugin and DB update.

placement.description: Whether this plugin runs before, after, or as a built-in. Default: post_builtin

order.description: Position within placement group. Lower values execute earlier. Default: 0

config.description: Configuration for the telemetry plugin (Prometheus metrics)

custom_labels.description: Custom labels to add to Prometheus metrics

push_gateway.description: Configuration for pushing metrics to a Prometheus Push Gateway for multi-node cluster deployments

enabled.description: Whether to enable pushing metrics to the Push Gateway

push_gateway_url.description: URL of the Prometheus Push Gateway (e.g., http://pushgateway:9091)

instance_id.description: Instance identifier for grouping metrics. If not set, hostname is used automatically.

push_interval.description: How often to push metrics in seconds

basic_auth.description: Basic authentication credentials for the Push Gateway

disable_content_logging.description: Disable logging of request and response content

logging_headers.description: List of headers to capture in log metadata

is_vk_mandatory.description: Whether virtual key (x-bf-vk header) is mandatory for all requests

required_headers.description: List of headers that must be present in requests

config.description: Configuration for the Maxim SDK integration plugin

api_key.description: API key for Maxim SDK authentication

log_repo_id.description: Optional default ID for the Maxim logger instance

config.description: Configuration for the semantic cache plugin

provider.description: Provider to use for generating embeddings. Required for semantic search; omit it for direct hash mode with dimension: 1.

keys.description: API keys for the embedding provider. These are injected at runtime for config-driven setups and are not needed for direct caching with dimension: 1.

embedding_model.description: Model to use for generating embeddings in provider-backed semantic caching. Required when provider is set and not allowed in direct-only mode.

cleanup_on_shutdown.description: Clean up cache on shutdown (default: false)

ttl.description: Time-to-live for cached responses (supports duration strings like '5m', '1h' or seconds as number, default: 5min)

threshold.description: Cosine similarity threshold for semantic matching (default: 0.8)

dimension.description: Dimension for vector store embeddings. Use 1 for direct (hash-based) caching without an embedding provider.

default_cache_key.description: Default cache key used when no per-request key is provided. When set, all requests without an explicit x-bf-cache-key header will use this value and be cached automatically.

conversation_history_threshold.description: Skip caching for requests with more than this number of messages in conversation history (default: 3)

cache_by_model.description: Include model in cache key (default: true)

cache_by_provider.description: Include provider in cache key (default: true)

exclude_system_prompt.description: Exclude system prompt in cache key (default: false)

service_name.description: Service name to be used for tracing

trace_type.description: Type of trace to use for the OTEL collector

protocol.description: Protocol to use for the OTEL collector

metrics_enabled.description: Enable push-based metrics export via OTLP. Recommended for multi-node cluster deployments.

metrics_endpoint.description: OTLP metrics endpoint URL (e.g., http://otel-collector:4318/v1/metrics for HTTP or otel-collector:4317 for gRPC)

headers.description: Custom headers for the collector. Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix for environment variable substitution.

tls_ca_cert.description: Path to TLS CA certificate file

insecure.description: Skip TLS verification (ignored if tls_ca_cert is set)

config.description: Configuration for the Datadog APM and metrics plugin

service_name.description: Name of the service to report to Datadog

agent_addr.description: Address of the Datadog Agent for APM traces

custom_tags.description: Additional tags to add to all traces and metrics

routing_target.description: A single weighted routing target within a rule. All fields except weight are optional; omitting provider or model means use the incoming request value. Weights across all targets in a rule must sum to 1.

provider.description: Target provider name (e.g., 'openai', 'azure'). Omit to use the incoming request provider

model.description: Target model name. Omit to use the incoming request model

key_id.description: Optional API key UUID to pin for this target. Omit for load-balanced key selection

weight.description: Probability weight for this target (must be > 0). All weights in a rule must sum to 1

routing_rule.description: Routing rule for dynamic provider/model selection

cel_expression.description: CEL (Common Expression Language) expression for rule evaluation

chain_rule.description: If true, re-evaluates routing chain after this rule matches

targets.description: Weighted routing targets. Weights must sum to 1. Omit provider or model to use the incoming request value.

scope_id.description: Entity ID for non-global scopes (required for non-global scope)

virtual_key_provider_config.description: Provider configuration for a virtual key

weight.description: Weight for load balancing (null opts out of weighted routing)

allowed_models.description: Allowed models for this provider config. Use ["*"] to allow all models; empty array denies all (deny-by-default).

key_ids.description: Key identifiers allowed for this provider config. Use ["*"] to allow all keys; empty array denies all (deny-by-default). In config.json, values are key names. Via the API, values are key UUIDs.

virtual_key_mcp_config.description: MCP configuration for a virtual key

mcp_client_name.description: MCP client name (config file format — resolved to mcp_client_id at startup)

tools_to_execute.description: Include-only list of tools this Virtual Key is permitted to execute from this MCP client. ['*'] means all tools allowed, [] means no tools allowed (deny-by-default).

auth_config.description: Authentication configuration. Deprecated: Use governance.auth_config instead.

disable_auth_on_inference.description: Whether authentication is disabled on inference

pricing_sync_interval.description: Pricing sync interval in seconds. Default is 24 hours. Minimum is 3600 seconds (1 hour).

base_url.description: Base URL for the provider (optional, required for Ollama)

extra_headers.description: Additional headers to send with requests

enforce_http2.description: Force HTTP/2 on provider connections (relevant for Bedrock and other net/http-based providers)

insecure_skip_verify.description: Disable TLS certificate verification for provider connections. This bypasses server certificate validation and should be used only as a last resort when a trusted CA chain cannot be configured. Prefer ca_cert_pem for self-signed or private CA deployments.

ca_cert_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for provider endpoint connections (e.g. self-signed or internal CA)

stream_idle_timeout_in_seconds.description: Idle timeout per stream chunk in seconds. If no data is received for this many seconds, the stream is closed. Default: 60.

max_conns_per_host.description: Maximum number of TCP connections per provider host. For HTTP/2 (e.g. Bedrock), each connection supports ~100 concurrent streams. Default: 5000.

beta_header_overrides.description: Override default Anthropic beta header support per provider. Keys are header prefixes (e.g. 'redact-thinking-'), values are true (supported) or false (unsupported). Headers not listed use the built-in defaults.

extra_headers.description: Additional headers to send with requests

enforce_http2.description: Force HTTP/2 on provider connections (relevant for Bedrock and other net/http-based providers)

insecure_skip_verify.description: Disable TLS certificate verification for provider connections. This bypasses server certificate validation and should be used only as a last resort when a trusted CA chain cannot be configured. Prefer ca_cert_pem for self-signed or private CA deployments.

ca_cert_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for provider endpoint connections (e.g. self-signed or internal CA)

stream_idle_timeout_in_seconds.description: Idle timeout per stream chunk in seconds. If no data is received for this many seconds, the stream is closed. Default: 60.

max_conns_per_host.description: Maximum number of TCP connections per provider host. For HTTP/2 (e.g. Bedrock), each connection supports ~100 concurrent streams. Default: 5000.

beta_header_overrides.description: Override default Anthropic beta header support per provider. Keys are header prefixes (e.g. 'redact-thinking-'), values are true (supported) or false (unsupported). Headers not listed use the built-in defaults.

disable_store.description: Disable OpenAI Responses API conversation storage.

value.description: API key value (can use env. prefix)

models.description: Models this key can access. Use ["*"] to allow all models; empty array denies all (deny-by-default).

use_for_batch_api.description: Whether this key can be used for batch API operations (default: false)

aliases.description: Model alias mappings: maps a model name to a provider-specific identifier (deployment name, inference profile ID, fine-tuned model ID, etc.)

access_key.description: AWS access key (can use env. prefix)

secret_key.description: AWS secret key (can use env. prefix)

session_token.description: AWS session token (can use env. prefix)

role_arn.description: AWS IAM role ARN for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

external_id.description: External ID for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

session_name.description: Role session name for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

batch_s3_config.description: S3 bucket configuration for Bedrock batch operations

prefix.description: S3 key prefix for batch files

is_default.description: Whether this is the default bucket for batch operations

url.description: VLLM server base URL (can use env. prefix)

model_name.description: Exact model name served on this VLLM instance

use_deployments_endpoint.description: Whether to use the deployments endpoint instead of the models endpoint (default: false)

url.description: Ollama server base URL (can use env. prefix)
SGLang server base URL (can use env. prefix)

project_id.description: Google Cloud project ID (can use env. prefix)

send_back_raw_request.description: Include raw request in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

send_back_raw_request.description: Include raw request in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

send_back_raw_request.description: Include raw request in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

send_back_raw_request.description: Include raw request in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

send_back_raw_request.description: Include raw request in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

send_back_raw_request.description: Include raw request in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

send_back_raw_request.description: Include raw request in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

send_back_raw_request.description: Include raw request in BifrostResponse (default: false)

send_back_raw_response.description: Include raw response in BifrostResponse (default: false)

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for internal logging only; strip from API responses returned to clients (default: false)

is_code_mode_client.description: Whether this client is a code mode client

connection_string.description: HTTP or SSE URL (alternative to http_config sub-object, required for HTTP or SSE connections)

oauth_config_id.description: OAuth config ID reference (required when auth_type is 'oauth' or 'per_user_oauth')

headers.description: Headers to send with the request (for headers auth type)

tools_to_execute.description: Include-only list of tools to execute. ['*'] means all, [] means none.

tools_to_auto_execute.description: Auto-execute list of tools. ['*'] means all, [] means none.

tool_sync_interval.description: Per-client override for tool sync interval (Go duration, e.g. '10m', '1h', 0 = use global, negative = disabled)

allowed_extra_headers.description: Allowlist of request-level headers that callers may forward to this MCP server at execution time. Use ['*'] to allow all headers.

is_ping_available.description: Whether the MCP server supports ping for health checks (default: true)

tool_pricing.description: Tool pricing map (tool name to cost per execution)

allow_on_all_virtual_keys.description: When true, this MCP server is accessible to all virtual keys without requiring explicit per-key assignment. All tools are allowed by default. If a virtual key has an explicit MCP config for this server, that config takes precedence and overrides this behaviour.

code_mode_binding_level.description: How tools are exposed in VFS for code execution

disable_auto_tool_inject.description: When true, MCP tools are not automatically injected into requests. Tools are only included when explicitly specified via request context filters or headers, such as x-bf-mcp-include-tools or x-bf-mcp-include-clients.

scheme.description: Weaviate server scheme (http or https) - REQUIRED

host.description: Weaviate server host (host:port). If host is without a port number then the 80 port for insecured and 443 port for secured connections will be used.

secured.description: Secured set it to true if it's a secured connection

headers.description: Additional headers to send with requests

class_name.description: Class name for Weaviate vector store

redis_config.description: Redis configuration for vector store (also used for Valkey-compatible endpoints)

addr.description: Redis/Valkey server address (host:port) - REQUIRED (can use env. prefix)

username.description: Username for Redis AUTH (optional, can use env. prefix)

password.description: Password for Redis AUTH (optional, can use env. prefix)

use_tls.description: Use TLS for the Redis/Valkey connection (optional)

insecure_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification for Redis/Valkey connections. Use only when a trusted CA cannot be configured.

ca_cert_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for Redis/Valkey TLS connections

cluster_mode.description: Use Redis Cluster mode. Required for cluster configuration endpoints; when enabled, db must be 0.

host.description: Qdrant server host - REQUIRED (can use env. prefix)

api_key.description: API key for authentication (optional, can use env. prefix)
Pinecone API key - REQUIRED (can use env. prefix)

index_host.description: Index host URL from Pinecone console - REQUIRED (e.g., your-index.svc.environment.pinecone.io)

ca_cert_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for TLS connections through the proxy (for SSL-intercepting proxies)

region.description: AWS region for cluster deployment (default: us-east-1)

failure_threshold.description: Number of failed probes before marking as failed

service_name.description: Service name used for discovery (used by consul, etcd, UDP, and mDNS)

bind_port.description: Port to bind for cluster communication

dial_timeout.description: Timeout for discovery dial operations in nanoseconds

allowed_address_space.description: CIDR notation for allowed address spaces (e.g., ['10.0.0.0/8', '192.168.0.0/16'])

k8s_label_selector.description: Kubernetes label selector for filtering pods

dns_names.description: DNS names to resolve for node discovery

consul_address.description: Consul server address for service discovery

mdns_service.description: mDNS service name for local network discovery

scim_config.description: SAML/SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) configuration

apiToken.description: Okta API token for Admin API access

userIdField.description: JWT claim field for user ID (default: 'sub')

teamIdsField.description: JWT claim field for team IDs (default: 'groups')

entra_config.description: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) JWT authentication configuration

tenantId.description: Azure tenant ID or 'common' for multi-tenant applications

clientSecret.description: Client secret (optional, required for token revocation)

cloud.description: Cloud environment: 'commercial' (default), 'gcc-high' for US Government GCC High, or 'dod' for Department of Defense

appIdUri.description: App ID URI for v1.0 tokens (e.g., api://{clientId})

userIdField.description: JWT claim field for user ID (default: 'oid')

teamIdsField.description: JWT claim field for team IDs (default: 'groups')

load_balancer_config.description: Load balancer configuration for intelligent request routing

tracker_config.description: Configuration for tracking route metrics and performance

bootstrap.description: Bootstrap data for initializing load balancer with historical metrics

guardrails_config.description: Guardrails configuration for content moderation and policy enforcement

description.description: Description of what the rule does

cel_expression.description: CEL (Common Expression Language) expression for rule evaluation

apply_to.description: When to apply the guardrail (input, output, or both)

sampling_rate.description: Percentage of requests to apply this rule to (0-100)

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for rule execution

provider_config_ids.description: IDs of provider configurations to use with this rule

id.description: Unique identifier for the provider config

policy_name.description: Name of the specific policy to use

enabled.description: Whether this provider config is enabled

timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds for provider execution

audit_logs_config.description: Audit logs configuration for CADF-compliant activity logging

disabled.description: Whether audit logging is disabled (default: false)

hmac_key.description: HMAC secret key for signing audit events (minimum 32 bytes). Can use env. prefix for environment variables (e.g., 'env.AUDIT_HMAC_KEY').

retention_days.description: Number of days to retain audit logs (0 means no retention limit)

large_payload_optimization.description: Large payload streaming optimization configuration. Detects large request/response payloads and streams them through without full materialization.

enabled.description: Master toggle for large payload optimization (default: false)

request_threshold_bytes.description: Request body size in bytes above which streaming kicks in (default: 10MB)

response_threshold_bytes.description: Response body size in bytes above which streaming kicks in (default: 10MB)

prefetch_size_bytes.description: How much of the request to prefetch for metadata extraction in bytes (default: 64KB)

max_payload_bytes.description: Maximum allowed payload size in bytes; reject above this (default: 500MB)

truncated_log_bytes.description: How many bytes to store as truncated preview in logs (default: 1MB)

websocket_config.description: Optional tuning for the WebSocket gateway (Responses API WebSocket Mode, Realtime API). WebSocket is always enabled; these fields override the high defaults.

max_connections_per_user.description: Maximum concurrent WebSocket connections per user

transcript_buffer_size.description: Number of transcript entries to buffer for Realtime API mid-session fallback

max_idle_per_key.description: Maximum idle connections per provider/key combination

max_total_connections.description: Maximum total idle connections across all providers

idle_timeout_seconds.description: Seconds before an idle connection is evicted

max_connection_lifetime_seconds.description: Maximum lifetime of a connection in seconds

provider_pricing_override.description: Scoped pricing override applied at runtime by the model catalog

virtual_key_id.description: Virtual key ID (required for virtual_key* scopes)

provider_key_id.description: Provider key ID (required for provider_key and virtual_key_provider_key scopes)

match_type.description: How the pattern is matched against model names

pattern.description: Model name pattern to match (exact name or wildcard prefix ending with *)

request_types.description: Request types this override applies to. At least one value is required.

custom_provider_config.description: Custom provider configuration for extending or customizing provider behavior

is_key_less.description: Whether the custom provider requires a key

request_path_overrides.description: Mapping of request type to custom path overriding the default provider path

allowed_requests.description: Allowed request types for the custom provider
